pattern: Track the index and side of the last commanded-or-enemy marker seen while scanning once; whenever two markers of opposite ownership meet with nothing but captured ground between them, that gap is a candidate answer.
hints: Only the positions holding your fort or an enemy fort matter as 'markers' -- an empty position never ends a capturable run by itself.

Walk the array once, remembering the index and type (yours or the enemy's) of the last marker you saw; whenever the current marker's type differs from the last, everything strictly between them is capturable.

Because you only advance the 'last marker' pointer on a non-empty cell, the region between two consecutive markers is automatically a solid run -- take the maximum such gap between opposite-type markers across the whole array.

edge_inputs.description: a single isolated capturable segment between opposite markers
several valid segments exist; the largest one is not the first one encountered
adjacent same-type markers bound no capturable segment even though zeros follow
two opposite markers with nothing between them capture nothing
single-position array holding only your own fort
single-position array holding only an empty destination
no fort under your command anywhere in the array
forts under your command exist but there is no empty destination anywhere

stress.description: random forts array over the full length range mixing all three cell types
